jokes.text: What am I sitting on? Top of the world? Dock of the bay? I'm out.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what they're sitting on, expecting literal answers
jokes.punchline: Gets song titles instead, then gives up
jokes.explanation: Quick wordplay misdirection - efficient but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Oh, undies!
jokes.setup: Someone discovers underwear in an inappropriate place
jokes.punchline: The awkward realization and exclamation
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy setup - the discovery moment is inherently funny

jokes.text: They're mine. See, Joey's. They're Joe-Joe-Joey's.
jokes.setup: Someone tries to claim ownership of underwear
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts themselves with stammering
jokes.explanation: Good character-based stammering - shows panic and guilt effectively

jokes.text: I don't know. I'm Joey. I'm disgusting. I take my underwear off in other people's homes.
jokes.setup: Asked why his underwear is there
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact self-assessment as disgusting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey characterization - his casual acceptance of being gross is funny and character-specific

jokes.text: Tighty-whiteys? What are you, 8?
jokes.setup: Someone wears tighty-whiteys
jokes.punchline: Joey's age-inappropriate assessment
jokes.explanation: Standard underwear humor - Joey's bluntness is in character but joke is predictable

jokes.text: I said it was mine, and that I was playing a woman in a play.
jokes.setup: Had to explain Rachel's razor in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Chandler's desperate lie about playing a woman
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the escalating lie sequence - Chandler's quick thinking under pressure

jokes.text: And around the ankles, that is a tough spot.
jokes.setup: Continuing the woman-in-play lie
jokes.punchline: Chandler getting specific about shaving technique
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Chandler doubling down with specific details makes the lie funnier

jokes.text: Yeah, actually.
jokes.setup: Joey sarcastically says sex makes lying easier
jokes.punchline: Monica's casual confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan timing - the economy and Monica's matter-of-fact delivery sells it

jokes.text: Meanwhile, I get Rachel shoving your underwear in my face...and asking when she can come see me star in Ugly Woman.
jokes.setup: The consequences of his earlier lie
jokes.punchline: The play title has evolved to 'Ugly Woman'
jokes.explanation: Great callback escalation - the lie has grown and now Rachel wants tickets. The title 'Ugly Woman' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: All right. But you do it with me once.
jokes.setup: Chandler agreeing to help them
jokes.punchline: His unexpected quid pro quo suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Chandler's frustrated proposition catches everyone off guard

jokes.text: Joey! -Didn't think so.
jokes.setup: Joey's shocked reaction to Chandler's suggestion
jokes.punchline: Chandler's resigned acceptance of rejection
jokes.explanation: Good beat comedy - Chandler's expectation of rejection is funny

jokes.text: Does anybody else just take off their underwear when they're hot?
jokes.setup: Trying to normalize his underwear situation
jokes.punchline: Joey's completely inappropriate question to the group
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey logic - his attempt to normalize bizarre behavior is perfectly in character

jokes.text: I really liked that Lamaze class I took.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions taking a literature class
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's random reference to Lamaze class
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe randomness - connecting unrelated educational experiences in her unique way

jokes.text: ...with a less painful final exam.
jokes.setup: Following up on Phoebe's Lamaze comment
jokes.punchline: Comparing literature exam to childbirth
jokes.explanation: Smart callback with good comparison - Rachel building on Phoebe's randomness effectively

jokes.text: What did the police say?
jokes.setup: Ross says someone ate his sandwich
jokes.punchline: Joey's assumption it's a crime worth reporting
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - Joey treating sandwich theft like a serious crime is perfectly absurd and in character

jokes.text: Someone ate the only good thing going on in my life!
jokes.setup: Ross explaining the sandwich situation
jokes.punchline: His pathetic life reduced to one sandwich
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ross's dramatic overstatement perfectly captures his current pathetic state

jokes.text: Look, I left a note and everything. 'Knock-knock.' 'Who's there?' 'Ross Geller's lunch.' 'Ross Geller's lunch, who?' 'Ross Geller's lunch. Please don't take me. Okay?'
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his preventative measures
jokes.punchline: His incredibly elaborate knock-knock joke note
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - Ross creating an elaborate knock-knock joke format for a simple 'don't eat this' note is perfectly neurotic and over-the-top

jokes.text: I'm surprised you didn't wear it home.
jokes.setup: Ross's obsession with his sandwich
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggesting Ross would wear it like clothing
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm - escalating Ross's attachment to absurd levels

jokes.text: What would you say, Phoebe? Stuff like: 'Keep your mitts off my grub.'
jokes.setup: Asked what tough street talk she'd use
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's adorably innocent attempt at street slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - her version of tough street talk is wholesome and childlike, completely in character

jokes.text: Do you picture Phoebe on the street with the entire cast of Annie?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's innocent street talk
jokes.punchline: Chandler imagining her with musical orphans
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy - perfectly captures how Phoebe's version of 'street tough' would look. The Annie reference is inspired.

jokes.text: Someday I'll tell you how I stabbed the cop. He stabbed me first!
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming to be tough
jokes.punchline: Her violent story with innocent justification
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe darkness - casually mentioning stabbing a cop but making it sound defensive and reasonable

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late, but I left late.
jokes.setup: Rachel apologizing for being late
jokes.punchline: Her circular non-explanation
jokes.explanation: Good economy - Rachel's non-explanation is perfectly circular and delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I started it, but then there was this pep rally. And I was on top of the pyramid.
jokes.setup: Asked if she read the book in high school
jokes.punchline: Rachel's priorities were cheerleading over literature
jokes.explanation: Good character work - perfectly captures Rachel's high school priorities and shallow nature

jokes.text: Symbolism!
jokes.setup: Teacher asks for theme analysis
jokes.punchline: Rachel blurts out the one literary term she knows
jokes.explanation: Great economy and character work - Rachel desperately grasping at the one thing she heard

jokes.text: You completely stole my answer!
jokes.setup: Phoebe gives a detailed analysis
jokes.punchline: Rachel's indignant accusation of theft
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Rachel's accusation when she clearly didn't understand the material

jokes.text: I think that yours is a question with many possible answers. Would you care to venture one?
jokes.setup: Teacher asks Rachel a direct question
jokes.punchline: Rachel's non-answer that turns question back on teacher
jokes.explanation: Clever deflection - Rachel's pseudo-intellectual non-answer is perfectly evasive

jokes.text: The best you got in high school was 'Wet Pants Geller.'
jokes.setup: Ross excited about his cool nickname 'Mental'
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing his embarrassing high school nickname
jokes.explanation: Great sibling dynamic - Monica deflating Ross's moment with embarrassing history. 'Wet Pants Geller' is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: It was the water fountain, okay?!
jokes.setup: His embarrassing nickname revealed
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive explanation makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Ross's explanation doesn't help his case and shows his lingering trauma

jokes.text: Hey, Tribbiani! Give me that coffee! Now!
jokes.setup: Ross talking about getting tough with people
jokes.punchline: His attempt to be tough with Joey
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate - Ross trying to be tough is inherently funny given his mild nature

jokes.text: I can't believe you thought you'd tape us having sex on the first date!
jokes.setup: Woman discovers video equipment
jokes.punchline: Her assumption about Joey's intentions
jokes.explanation: Setup for the misunderstanding - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm Joey. I mean, I'm disgusting. I make low-budget adult films.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain the video equipment
jokes.punchline: Joey's self-introduction pattern with escalating confession
jokes.explanation: Great callback structure - Joey's self-introduction pattern escalates to porn, maintaining his character voice

jokes.text: Tell them you had to make an adult film for your...adult film class.
jokes.setup: Trying to help Joey with an explanation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ridiculous academic justification
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - Chandler's attempt to make porn sound academic is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Her secret is, she puts an extra slice of gravy-soaked bread in the middle. I call it 'The Moistmaker.'
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his sister's sandwich technique
jokes.punchline: The gross but specific name 'Moistmaker'
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment - 'Moistmaker' became a cultural reference. Ross's specificity about food is perfectly neurotic and the name is brilliantly gross.

jokes.text: Did you confuse it with your own turkey sandwich with a Moistmaker?
jokes.setup: Boss claiming it was a simple mistake
jokes.punchline: Ross's increasingly specific and sarcastic questioning
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Ross using the Moistmaker specificity to prove the boss is lying. His rage building is perfect.

jokes.text: You threw my sandwich away? My sandwich?!
jokes.setup: Learning his sandwich was thrown in trash
jokes.punchline: Ross's mounting fury and repetition
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - the repetition and building rage perfectly sets up Ross's breakdown

jokes.text: First, you'd think she's a woman, but she's not. She's a cyborg.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asked to explain Jane Eyre
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong sci-fi interpretation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - Phoebe confidently turning classic literature into sci-fi is perfectly unexpected and hilarious

jokes.text: Well, feminism, yes. But also the robots.
jokes.setup: Teacher mentions feminism in Jane Eyre
jokes.punchline: Rachel doubling down on Phoebe's robot theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Rachel confidently building on Phoebe's absurd premise in front of the teacher

jokes.text: But I definitely don't care about my sandwich.
jokes.setup: Ross on tranquilizers after his rage
jokes.punchline: His drugged claim about not caring
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Ross medicated into not caring about the thing that drove him crazy

jokes.text: It was so funny when you compared Jane Eyre to Robocop.
jokes.setup: Phoebe apologizing for misleading Rachel
jokes.punchline: Revealing Rachel's classroom comparison got even more specific
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Rachel didn't just say robots, she specifically said Robocop, making it even more absurd

jokes.text: Let's give our friend a chance to explain why he's such a big pervert!
jokes.setup: Trying to help Joey with the photo situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's 'helpful' framing makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm - his attempt to help actually makes Joey sound worse

jokes.text: Joey's a sex addict. What?! No, I'm not! It's okay! It's good! It's a disease.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to help Joey
jokes.punchline: His explanation makes Joey sound worse, Joey's panicked denial
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Chandler's 'help' keeps making things worse, Joey's denial is perfectly panicked

jokes.text: Well, let's see what everybody thinks of that.
jokes.setup: Joey announces he slept with Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica's casual invitation for group judgment
jokes.explanation: Good beat - Monica's casual reaction to the bombshell announcement

jokes.text: I guess I wanted to keep it...as a souvenir.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she kept Joey's underwear
jokes.punchline: Monica's creepy romantic justification
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Monica making the underwear thing sound romantic is appropriately awkward

jokes.text: She has been trying to get me back in the sack ever since London!
jokes.setup: Building the fake story about Monica pursuing him
jokes.punchline: Joey painting Monica as the aggressor
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalating lie - Joey making Monica the desperate one

jokes.text: I'm Monica. I'm disgusting. I stalk guys and keep their underpants.
jokes.setup: Monica adopting Joey's self-description pattern
jokes.punchline: Monica's version of the 'I'm disgusting' speech
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure - Monica perfectly mimics Joey's self-introduction pattern but makes it about stalking behavior

jokes.text: I'm only eating the skin, so the chicken's up for grabs.
jokes.setup: Joey with a bucket of chicken
jokes.punchline: His disgusting eating habits casually offered to share
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - his gross food habits and generous spirit combined perfectly

jokes.text: Oh, yeah! We should have essay questions!
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting tests for the class
jokes.punchline: Monica's over-enthusiasm for academic rigor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Monica's competitive nature extending to casual literature class
